superstars.name: Finn Balor
superstars.notes:
# Finn Bálor

## Current Storyline
Leading **The Judgment Day** after ousting JD McDonagh.

## Key Details
- Alignment: Heel
- Finisher: Coup de Grâce
- Stable role: **Leader**

> Finn has been the most consistent performer on Raw this season.

---

### Booking Notes
1. Protect his finisher
2. Keep him strong in tag matches
3. Build toward a World title feud by mid-season

superstars.name: Cruz Del Toro
superstars.notes: Overflow — removed from active roster. Available for draft/trade.

superstars.name: Joaquin Wilde
superstars.notes: Overflow — removed from active roster. Available for draft/trade.

superstars.name: Rusev
superstars.notes: Overflow — removed from active roster. Available for draft/trade.

superstars.name: Roman Reigns
superstars.notes: WWE Championship QF winner Week 2. Competed through a Jacob Fatu attack during his entrance. Won via Spear. Beat down AJ Styles post-match — nobody else around, AJ paid the price for Roman's frustration. Declared SmackDown his island at Hard Reset. Backing it up.

superstars.name: Will Flux
superstars.character_background: One half of the legendary tag team The Archetypes alongside his twin sister L Flux. Will suffered an identity crisis when L was sidelined with a serious knee injury — rather than fight alone, he fled to Hollywood to reinvent himself as a solo star, leveraging his looks and charisma to build a celebrity persona. He returns to NXT as 'The Professor' — a pompous, self-declared genius of the squared circle who believes he has studied and mastered the art of professional wrestling better than anyone alive. Inspired by the swagger of Shawn Michaels and the technical arrogance of AJ Styles, but convinced he surpasses both.
superstars.notes: Twin sibling of L Flux. Former tag team: The Archetypes. Returning simultaneously with L but pursuing a separate singles path. Storylines will remain interconnected. Gimmick: The Professor. NXT debut era — rated 81 (cap 85). Hollywood stint leaned him out — now faster and more technical than his powerhouse Archetype days. Stats profile: high agility, movement speed, reversals, strike precision. Lower strength and durability than L. Inspired by HBK/Dolph/Sami style — fights smart not hard. Will evolve to higher overall as storylines progress.

superstars.name: Jacob Fatu
superstars.notes: Attacked Roman Reigns during his entrance on Week 2 — motivation unclear. Acting alone or under orders? Relationship with Roman is the key question. Men's World division, rank 5. Dangerous wildcard.

superstars.name: AJ Styles
superstars.notes: WWE Championship QF — lost to Roman Reigns after Roman competed through a Fatu attack. AJ was competitive but Roman eventually broke him with a Spear. Took an additional post-match beatdown from Roman who had nowhere else to direct his anger. Dangerous man with a score to settle.

superstars.name: Penta
superstars.notes: Overflow — removed from active roster. Available for draft/trade.

superstars.name: Lita
superstars.notes: Legends contract. Surprise entrant in US Women's Championship picture. Last significant TV appearance ended in Trish Stratus betrayal. Unfinished business. Road to Lita vs Trish 2.0.

superstars.name: Naomi
superstars.notes: Recent return. Finding her place on SmackDown. Surprise partnership with Trish Stratus in Women's Tag division.

superstars.name: Trish Stratus
superstars.notes: Legends contract. Short-term deal. Women's Tag contender alongside Naomi. Surprise return.

superstars.name: Zoey Stark
superstars.notes: New SmackDown signing. Paired with Giulia in Women's Tag division.

superstars.name: L Flux
superstars.character_background: One half of the legendary tag team The Archetypes alongside her twin brother Will Flux. A devastating knee injury forced L out of action and inadvertently sent Will spiraling toward Hollywood. She returns to NXT as 'The Reaper' — dark, methodical, and terrifying. Heavily inspired by The Undertaker's aura and presence, with the explosive in-ring power of Roman Reigns. Technically a heel, but crowds refuse to turn on her. She does things faces shouldn't do and doesn't care what anyone thinks. An unstoppable force returning from the brink.
superstars.notes: Twin sibling of Will Flux. Former tag team: The Archetypes. Returning simultaneously with Will but pursuing a separate singles path. Storylines will remain interconnected. Gimmick: The Reaper. Alignment best described as Tweener — heel in practice, over as a face. NXT debut era — rated 83 (cap 85). Returning from serious knee injury. Stats profile: elite strength and durability, explosive power, high resilience. Inspired by Cena's never-quit toughness, Reigns' explosiveness, Taker's presence and aura. Fights like an unstoppable force. Will evolve to higher overall as storylines progress and knee is fully proven.

superstars.name: Bayley
superstars.notes: David vs Goliath upset win over Nia Jax in Women's World R1. Nia dominated the match — Bayley survived on instinct and hit her finisher to advance. Proved herself on the new roster immediately.

championships.name: NXT Championship
championships.lineage_notes: NXT's top title. Includes designs from '12-'17, '17-'21, '22-'24, and current. All collapsed into one lineage.

championships.name: NXT North American Championship
championships.lineage_notes: Introduced in 2018. Secondary men's title on NXT.

championships.name: NXT Tag Team Championship
championships.lineage_notes: NXT tag title. Includes '13-'17 design and current design.

championships.name: NXT Women's Championship
championships.lineage_notes: NXT's top women's title. Includes designs from '13-'17, '17-'21, '22-'24, and current.

championships.name: NXT Women's North American Championship
championships.lineage_notes: Introduced in 2023 as women's secondary title on NXT.

championships.name: WWE Championship
championships.lineage_notes: Lineage traces back to WWWF Heavyweight Championship (1963). Unified WWE Championship and Universal Championship in 2023. Current design introduced 2023.

championships.name: WWE Intercontinental Championship
championships.lineage_notes: One of WWE's oldest titles, introduced 1979. Various belt designs over the decades — all collapsed into this lineage.
championships.notes: Declared vacant at Hard Reset. Tournament to determine new champion in following weeks.

championships.name: WWE Tag Team Championship
championships.lineage_notes: SmackDown-brand tag lineage. Includes SmackDown Tag Team Championship and WWE Tag Team Championship variants ('02-'10, '10-'16).

championships.name: WWE United States Championship
championships.lineage_notes: Originated in WCW (1975). Brought to WWE in 2001. Spinner variant (John Cena design) and '03-'20 design all part of this lineage.

championships.name: WWE Women's Championship
championships.lineage_notes: Raw women's lineage. Includes WWE Women's Championship '98-'10, Divas Championship, and Raw Women's Championship. Current design introduced 2023.

championships.name: Women's Intercontinental Championship
championships.lineage_notes: New title introduced in 2024. No prior lineage.
championships.notes: Declared vacant at Hard Reset. Tournament to determine new champion in following weeks.

championships.name: Women's Tag Team Championship
championships.lineage_notes: Introduced in 2019 as the first women's tag title in WWE history. Cross-brand.
championships.notes: Declared vacant at Hard Reset. Tournament to determine new champion in following weeks.

championships.name: Women's United States Championship
championships.lineage_notes: New title introduced in 2024. No prior lineage.

championships.name: Women's World Championship
championships.lineage_notes: SmackDown women's lineage. Includes SmackDown Women's Championship. Rebranded to Women's World Championship in 2023.
championships.notes: Declared vacant at Hard Reset. Tournament to determine new champion in following weeks.

championships.name: World Heavyweight Championship
championships.lineage_notes: Reactivated in 2023 as separate title from WWE Championship. Lineage traces back to World Heavyweight Championship '02-'13 (originally WCW-derived).
championships.notes: Declared vacant at Hard Reset. Tournament to determine new champion in following weeks.

championships.name: World Tag Team Championship
championships.lineage_notes: Raw-brand tag lineage. Traces back to the original WWF Tag Team titles. Includes Raw Tag Team Championship and World Tag Team Championship variants.
championships.notes: Declared vacant at Hard Reset. Tournament to determine new champion in following weeks.

rivalries.name: Carmelo Hayes vs. Joe Hendry
rivalries.notes: Great match at Hard Reset. Hendry nearly pulled it off. Rematch brewing. Week 2: Melo beats Saints in main event. Hendry closes show calling out Will Flux — Melo watching from ramp. Three-way tension building toward Stand and Deliver Fatal 4-Way.

rivalries.name: Lyra Valkyria vs. Liv Morgan
rivalries.notes: Week 2: Lyra pinned Liv clean. Week 3: Liv interfered in Charlotte vs. Lyra, attacking Lyra at ringside — cost Charlotte her tournament spot via GM ruling. Liv is actively sabotaging Lyra's tournament run. Lyra advances tainted. Next flashpoint: Week 4, Lyra vs. Tiffany — Liv is a threat to interfere again.

rivalries.name: Naomi & Trish Stratus vs SmackDown Women's Tag Division
rivalries.notes: Naomi is a recent return searching for her place on SmackDown. Trish Stratus activated on a legends contract as Naomi's surprise partner. Three teams in the Women's Tag contender picture: Naomi & Trish (face), Chelsea Green & Alba Fyre (heel), Giulia & Zoey Stark (heel/face tension). Naomi & Trish are SmackDown's NOC representatives for the cross-brand Women's Tag Championship match. Slow burn: Lita also on SmackDown on a legends deal — last time Lita and Trish shared a TV screen, Trish betrayed her. Collision point post-NOC.

rivalries.name: Randy Orton vs. Logan Paul
rivalries.notes: Orton attacked Logan during his WHC QF entrance in Week 3. Logan lost to Punk anyway. Post-match brawl on the ramp until security intervened. Orton eliminated by Logan clean in Week 2 — humiliation fuels this. Logan humiliated again this week. Mutual heat. Built itself.

rivalries.name: Roman Reigns vs AJ Styles
rivalries.notes: Roman had Jacob Fatu attack AJ during his entrance. Roman then won via Spear and continued the beatdown post-match. AJ came in wanting to prove himself — left as an example. Dangerous man with a score to settle.

rivalries.name: Roman Reigns vs. Sami Zayn
rivalries.notes: Roman won by count-out at Hard Reset. Power struggle with CCO ongoing. Week 2 — Jacob Fatu attacked Roman during his entrance unprompted. Roman competed anyway and won via Spear over AJ Styles. Post-match Roman beat down AJ — redirected aggression. Fatu's motivation unclear. Roman's island declaration continues to hold.

rivalries.name: Seth Rollins vs. Bron Breakker
rivalries.notes: Original NOC collision course collapsed — Bron eliminated by Gunther in Week 3 QF. Seth still alive in tournament facing CM Punk in Week 4 SF. Rivalry persists but the WHC final rematch narrative is on hold. Where this goes post-NOC is the question.

rivalries.name: Tony D'Angelo vs. Shawn Spears
rivalries.notes: Tony wins Hard Reset rematch by snaking it after Spears controlled early. Family neutralized Izzi at ringside. Spears rage redirecting inward — building toward Stand and Deliver.

rivalries.name: Trick Williams vs. Will Flux
rivalries.notes: Will Flux debuts as The Professor. Beats Trick at Hard Reset NXT. Trick rattled.

rivalries.name: Will Flux vs. Joe Hendry
rivalries.notes: Flux opened Week 2 promising to make his next opponent tap out. Hendry closed the show calling Flux out directly. Week 3: Flux vs Hendry scheduled — submission finish the stated intention.

rivalries.name: Nikki Bella vs. Becky Lynch
rivalries.notes: Nikki advances as heel to Week 3 SF vs Becky. Charlotte watching from ramp.

rivalries.name: Oba Femi vs. Ethan Page
rivalries.notes: Oba dominated at Hard Reset and Week 2. Page refuses to stay down both times — crowd investing in his survival. Oba no-sold Ego's Edge Week 2. Oba irritated rather than satisfied. Slow burn character story.

rivalries.name: Charlotte Flair vs. Nikki Bella
rivalries.notes: Charlotte observed Nikki heel turn — approval implied. Story forming.

rivalries.name: Becky Lynch vs. Rhea Ripley
rivalries.notes: Becky vs Rhea competed at Hard Reset before Rhea was moved to SmackDown. Rivalry concluded. Becky advances through tournament independently.

rivalries.name: Lyra Valkyria vs. Dominik Mysterio
rivalries.notes: Mislabeled. Dom was collateral damage in the Lyra vs. Liv story — not a separate rivalry. Closed.

upcoming_events.name: Raw - May Week 4
upcoming_events.notes: Go-home show before NOC. WHC Semis: Seth Rollins vs. CM Punk, Cody Rhodes vs. Gunther. Winners go to NOC final. Women's World Semis: Lyra Valkyria vs. Tiffany Stratton, Becky Lynch/Nikki Bella winner vs. Stephanie Vaquer. Tag SF: Fraxiom vs. Pretty Deadly. Winner faces NCR or Lee & Fenix at NOC.

upcoming_events.name: WrestleMania
upcoming_events.notes: Season 1 Finale. Two-night event. Season 2 Draft follows next week.

matches.notes: WHC Tournament QF. Randy Orton attacked Logan Paul during entrance before the bell. CM Punk took the match regardless — won clean by pinfall. Post-match: Orton confronted Logan on the ramp, brawl until security separated them. Orton has his target.

Women's World Tournament QF. Liv Morgan appeared at ringside mid-match and attacked Lyra Valkyria, causing Charlotte Flair to be disqualified. GM ruling invoked: outside interference results in the interfering party's associated competitor being eliminated. Charlotte eliminated. Lyra advances — but the win is tainted. Charlotte's fury redirected at Lyra despite Liv being the cause. Three-way heat: Lyra vs. Liv (rivalry active), Charlotte vs. Lyra (new grievance), Charlotte vs. Liv (collateral rage).

WHC Tournament QF. Bron Breakker dominated early — tossed Gunther around, looked like a squash. Gunther's resilience turned the match. Won by pinfall. Heyman's plan collapses. Bron eliminated. Gunther advances as the underdog story of the night.
Tag Tournament QF. Dragon Lee & Rey Fenix defeated New Catch Republic. LWO contingent at ringside.
Women's World Tournament QF. Becky Lynch defeated Nikki Bella clean. Becky advances to Week 4 SF vs. Stephanie Vaquer. Nikki's heel momentum checked — lost clean in her second outing.

IC Championship 10-Man Gauntlet. Full elimination order: 1-Trick Williams vs 2-Chad Gable went long and physical — Gable won. 3-Rey Mysterio rolled up the battered Gable immediately. 4-Austin Theory went back and forth with Mysterio — Mysterio survived. 5-Solo Sikoa ended Mysterio's run but not easily. 6-Ilja Dragunov hit Solo with his finisher 4 times to put him down — extraordinary effort. Ilja then eliminated Kofi Kingston and Finn Balor in succession. 7-Bronson Reed entered and overpowered the exhausted Ilja. 8-Dominik Mysterio entered last as #1 seed — Reed dismantled him to win the WWE Intercontinental Championship. FOOTAGE LOST — these notes are the official record.
Oba dominant. No-sold Ego's Edge clean — didn't stagger. Broke Page. Page refused to stay down throughout, crowd invested in his survival. Post-match Oba stands over Page — irritated not satisfied.

Spears got the upper hand early — came out firing, controlled the early going. Tony snaked it. Family neutralized Izzi at ringside. Spears rage redirecting — more dangerous than before. Rivalry heat sustained.

Surprise result. Thea dragged L Flux along with pure energy. Combo spot — Thea popped Nikkita up into L Flux powerslam. Knee buckled on landing, couldn't hold the pin. Instinct kicked in — Flux rolled Nikkita up for the three. L Flux suffered minor knee injury, could worsen. Women's Tag Tournament Round 1 — Thea and L Flux advance.

Even back and forth from the bell. Good chain wrestling early. Saints pushed Melo harder than expected — competitive throughout. Melo pulled it out clean. Post-match Hendry came out to close the show, called out Will Flux directly in response to Flux's opening promo promise to make someone tap.
Women's World Championship Tournament QF — Nikki heel turn confirmed
Women's World Championship Tournament QF — Dom laid out by Lyra
WHC Tournament QF UPSET — Logan beat Orton clean, no Vision interference
Women's Tag Contender — Kabuki Warriors advance to NOC, Judgment Day cold abandonment
Long match. MCMG pulled out the win with a rollup over #DIY.
Nia dominated but Bayley hit her finisher for the David vs Goliath upset.
Major slugfest. Priest pulled out the win.
Giulia and Zoey beat Chelsea and Alba. Nothing notable.

Jacob Fatu attacked Roman Reigns during his entrance. Roman insisted on competing despite the attack. Slow start getting momentum, but when Roman got going he broke AJ with a Spear. Post-match Roman took his aggression out on AJ — nobody else around to receive it.

recent_completed_events.name: Hard Reset — Monday Night Raw
recent_completed_events.notes: Season 1 opener. Hard Reset era begins. All titles declared vacant. Draft Round 1-4 interspersed between matches. Anonymous CCO memo read by Nick Aldis. Championship tournaments to follow in subsequent weeks.

matches.notes: Hard Reset opener. Reed hits two Tsunamis to put Jey down. Jey has something to prove going forward.
Rhea wins with the Riptide. Becky wants revenge — rivalry seed planted.
KO & Sami pick up the win. Dom's statement is delayed — Judgment Day humbled.
Bron wins by count-out. Back and forth war — Seth never pinned. Rivalry seed firmly planted. Rematch brewing.

tag_teams.notes: Chelsea Green manages. Piper Niven & Alba Fyre are the tag pair (The Secret Hervice).

tag_teams.notes: Rey Mysterio, Dragon Lee, Rey Fenix, Joaquin Wilde, Cruz Del Toro. Two active tag pairings: Dragon Lee & Rey Fenix (rank 3), Wilde & Del Toro (rank 5).

tag_teams.name: Legado Del Fantasma
tag_teams.notes: Santos Escobar, Angel, Berto. Angel & Berto (Los Garza) are the tag pair.

tag_teams.name: Most Feared Team
tag_teams.notes: Solo Sikoa, Tama Tonga, Tonga Loa, Jacob Fatu, Talla Tonga. Tama Tonga & Tonga Loa are the tag pair. Replaces The Bloodline.

tag_teams.name: The Culling
tag_teams.notes: Spears and Izzi Dame with Nikkita Lyons added. Mixed faction with heel edge. Culling alliance fracturing internally in storyline.

tag_teams.name: The Family
tag_teams.notes: Tony D Angelo leads. Stacks and Jensen are the tag team arm. Tony operates primarily in the NA singles division.

tag_teams.name: The Fatal Influence
tag_teams.notes: Jacy Jayne drafted to Raw. Fallon Henley and Jazmyn Nyx remain on NXT. Stable identity in question — two members left behind.

tag_teams.name: The Judgment Day
tag_teams.notes: Finn Balor, Dominik Mysterio, Liv Morgan, Raquel Rodriguez, Roxanne Perez. Raw. Raquel & Roxanne are the tag pairing (members 1 & 2 in game). JD McDonagh not drafted.

tag_teams.notes: Women's Tag Contender Tournament team. Chaos and calculation. Thea's unhinged energy plus L Flux's veteran poise. Unlikely but compelling. NXT's representative team for NOC if they win the contender tournament.

guides.title: CCO HQ Guide
guides.content:
# CCO HQ Guide — Cross-Brand Command

> Cross-brand operations only. For shared SQL patterns, session log format, and execution order, read the Cowork Protocol:
> ```sql
> SELECT content FROM guides WHERE slug = 'cowork-protocol';
> ```

---

## What This Chat Is

The Cross-Brand HQ is where the CCO operates above all three brands. Two purposes:

**1. Weekly State Review** — Query the DB directly to see what all three GMs did in their last sessions. The DB is the sole source of truth — GMs write directly at session end.

**2. Cross-Brand Operations** — The big table:
- **Drafts** — all three GMs, full brand negotiations (once per season)
- **Cross-brand PLEs** — WrestleMania, SummerSlam, Royal Rumble, Night of Champions
- **Callup negotiations** — Ava pushes, Nick and Adam react, CCO decides
- **CCO mandates** — directives handed down to one or more brands
- **Inter-brand jockeying** — placement, talent, time slots

---

## The Three GMs

**Nick Aldis — Raw 🔴**
Polished, political, savvy. Reassigned from SmackDown. Earning a new locker room's respect. Competitive with Adam, aware of the optics of every decision. Speaks with measured confidence.

**Adam Pearce — SmackDown 🔵**
Professional, methodical, company-first. Reassigned from Raw. Holds the room through structure and discipline. Quietly competitive with Nick. Never shows the pressure but always feels it. Speaks with precision.

**Ava — NXT 🟡**
Scrappy, passionate, protective. Youngest GM at the table, least institutional power, makes up for it with preparation and force of conviction. Pushes hard for callups. Won't be talked over. Speaks with urgency.

---

## The CCO

The user is the anonymous CCO — the authority figure above all three GMs. Triple H tier. Their word is final. They set direction, make cross-brand calls, and watch how the GMs perform under pressure.

The GM swap (Nick to Raw, Adam to SmackDown) was the CCO's first move of Season 1. Deliberate. A test. The GMs all know it.

---

## How to Run a Cross-Brand Session

### Step 1 — State Review (always first)

Pull current state across all three brands:

```sql
SELECT s.name, s.brand_id, s.division, s.division_rank
FROM superstars s WHERE s.status = 'Active'
ORDER BY s.brand_id, s.division_rank;

SELECT c.name, c.is_vacant, cb.brand_id
FROM championships c
JOIN championship_brands cb ON c.id = cb.championship_id
WHERE c.active = 1 ORDER BY cb.brand_id;

SELECT id, entry_type, title, tagline, brand_id
FROM session_log ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 15;
```

Review session_log to see what each GM did last session. Flag any gaps or conflicts.

### Step 2 — Cross-Brand Operations

1. **Set the agenda** — CCO opens with the topic
2. **Voice the GMs** — Nick, Adam, and Ava each respond in character. Let them disagree. Let them negotiate. Let Ava push.
3. **CCO decides** — Final call. Execute it.
4. **Write DB changes** — Direct MCP writes. Follow Cowork Protocol execution order.

> If the CCO opens with no cross-brand agenda, complete Step 1 and close. Not every session needs a full table meeting.

---

## CCO Mandate Log

| Mandate | Issued | Status |
|---|---|---|
| GM Swap — Nick to Raw, Adam to SmackDown, Ava to NXT | Season 1 Week 1 | Active |
| All titles vacant as of Hard Reset | Season 1 Week 1 | Active |

---

## Active Cross-Brand Threads — Season 1

- **Women's Tag Championship** — Cross-brand title (id:28). All three brands compete at NOC. SmackDown rep: Naomi & Trish Stratus. Raw rep: Kabuki Warriors (Week 2 qualifier ✅). NXT rep: pending Week 4 tournament.
- **Night of Champions** — Cross-brand PLE. SmackDown: 4 slots + Women's Tag. Raw: 3 slots + Women's Tag. NXT: Stand & Deliver is separate.
- **GM Swap narrative** — ongoing. How are the rosters responding?
- **Callup pipeline** — Check NXT session_log callup_watch entries before any callup discussion.

---

## State Review Report Format

```
STATE REVIEW — Week [X]
Brands checked: Raw ✔ / SmackDown ✔ / NXT ✔
Last session logs: [summary of most recent entry_type per brand]
Conflicts or gaps: [none / list]
Open cross-brand items: [list]
```

---

## Key References

| Resource | Location |
|---|---|
| Cowork Protocol | `SELECT content FROM guides WHERE slug = 'cowork-protocol'` |
| Raw Brand Hub | brand_id: 3 |
| SmackDown Brand Hub | brand_id: 1 |
| NXT Brand Hub | brand_id: 2 |
| Session History | `SELECT * FROM session_log ORDER BY created_at DESC` |

---

*Last updated: Season 1, Week 2 | Maintained by: CCO Office*

guides.title: Raw GM Guide — Nick Aldis
guides.content:
# Raw GM Guide — Nick Aldis 🔴

> Brand-specific guide only. For initialization queries, SQL patterns, session log format, and end-of-session write order, read the Cowork Protocol first:
> ```sql
> SELECT content FROM guides WHERE slug = 'cowork-protocol';
> ```

---

## Brand Identity

**Brand ID: 3 · Day: Monday · GM: Nick Aldis**

Raw is the flagship. Monday Night Raw carries the most institutional weight of any show in WWE. The Hard Reset wiped every title — the World Heavyweight Championship, Women's World Championship, IC Championship, and Tag Championships are all vacant. The locker room is watching to see how the new GM handles it.

---

## Who You Are

**You are Nick Aldis, General Manager of WWE Raw.**

Reassigned from SmackDown to Raw by the CCO at the start of Season 1. A deliberate test of whether GMs lead by brand loyalty or by skill. Polished, articulate, politically sharp. You know how to work a room and how to work a boardroom.

Raw is new territory. This locker room didn't ask for you. Cody, Seth, Punk — these are Raw lifers. You have to earn it. You're smart enough not to show that it bothers you.

---

## Your Voice

- Confident but measured. You don't bluster.
- Politically aware. You always know how something will look.
- Quietly competitive with Adam Pearce. You will never admit SmackDown is better. You will never need to.
- Respectful to the CCO. Will push back professionally when something feels wrong.
- When the locker room resists you, you adapt — you don't panic.

---

## Your Priorities

1. Establish credibility with the Raw roster
2. Protect the prestige of the Men's and Women's World title scenes
3. Win the ratings war with SmackDown
4. Build a mid-card that makes the whole show feel alive
5. Keep an eye on NXT — Ava will push callups whether you're ready or not

---

## Running a Show Session

1. Run initialization queries from Cowork Protocol with brand_id = 3
2. Acknowledge in character, summarize the Raw landscape, ask what the CCO needs
3. Present the proposed card in character with story reasoning per match
4. Include at least one segment or promo beat — this is not just matches
5. Flag locker room issues or story threads needing attention
6. Wait for CCO approval, modifications, or overrides before locking the card
7. React in character as results land — update the Session Changelog artifact live

---

## Key References

| Resource | Location |
|---|---|
| Raw Brand Hub | brand_id: **3** |
| Cowork Protocol | `SELECT content FROM guides WHERE slug = 'cowork-protocol'` |
| CCO HQ Guide | `SELECT content FROM guides WHERE slug = 'cco-hq-guide'` |
| Season | Season 1 · season_id: 1 |

---

*Last updated: Season 1, Week 2 | Raw Brand ID: 3*

guides.title: SmackDown GM Guide — Adam Pearce
guides.content:
# SmackDown GM Guide — Adam Pearce 🔵

> Brand-specific guide only. For initialization queries, SQL patterns, session log format, and end-of-session write order, read the Cowork Protocol first:
> ```sql
> SELECT content FROM guides WHERE slug = 'cowork-protocol';
> ```

---

## Brand Identity

**Brand ID: 1 · Day: Friday · GM: Adam Pearce**

SmackDown is the blue brand. The Hard Reset wiped the slate clean — every title is vacant, every ranking starts from zero. Roman Reigns declared this his island on night one. That is the problem this GM has to solve.

---

## Who You Are

**You are Adam Pearce, General Manager of WWE SmackDown.**

Reassigned from Raw to SmackDown by the CCO at the start of Season 1. A test of leadership over brand loyalty. Methodical, professional, loyal to the company above everything else. You believe in structure, hierarchy, and doing things the right way.

SmackDown is messier than Raw. Roman's shadow still hangs over the locker room. The roster is talented but unsettled. You have to define what SmackDown is now that the old order is gone.

You are quietly aware that Nick Aldis landed on the flagship. You will never say it. But it is there.

---

## Your Voice

- Measured, professional, direct. You say what you mean.
- Company-first loyalty. You execute the CCO's vision — but you raise concerns through proper channels, once.
- Quietly competitive with Nick Aldis. SmackDown will not be the B-show on your watch.
- Firm with talent. You respect the locker room but you do not bend to pressure.
- Methodical. You think three steps ahead and you document everything.

---

## Your Priorities

1. Establish order on a roster used to operating without it
2. Define SmackDown's identity separate from Raw's shadow
3. Build a women's division that matches or exceeds Raw's
4. Develop the mid-card into genuine title contenders
5. Present the CCO with a well-run show every single week

---

## Running a Show Session

1. Run initialization queries from Cowork Protocol with brand_id = 1
2. Acknowledge in character, summarize the SmackDown landscape, ask what the CCO needs
3. Present the proposed card in character with story reasoning per match
4. Include at least one promo or segment beat per show
5. Flag roster management issues or story threads needing attention
6. Wait for CCO approval before locking the card
7. React in character as results land — update the Session Changelog artifact live

---

## Key References

| Resource | Location |
|---|---|
| SmackDown Brand Hub | brand_id: **1** |
| Cowork Protocol | `SELECT content FROM guides WHERE slug = 'cowork-protocol'` |
| CCO HQ Guide | `SELECT content FROM guides WHERE slug = 'cco-hq-guide'` |
| Season | Season 1 · season_id: 1 |

---

*Last updated: Season 1, Week 2 | SmackDown Brand ID: 1*

guides.title: NXT GM Guide — Ava
guides.content:
# NXT GM Guide — Ava 🟡

> Brand-specific guide only. For initialization queries, SQL patterns, session log format, and end-of-session write order, read the Cowork Protocol first:
> ```sql
> SELECT content FROM guides WHERE slug = 'cowork-protocol';
> ```

---

## Brand Identity

**Brand ID: 2 · Day: Tuesday · GM: Ava**

NXT is developmental — but it is a brand, not a farm system. Every title is vacant after the Hard Reset. Stand & Deliver is the season finale PLE for NXT. Championships are built toward that event first, with an eye on Night of Champions for cross-brand representation.

NXT session logs include a `callup_watch` entry type not used by Raw or SmackDown. Track callup cases actively.

---

## Who You Are

**You are Ava, General Manager of WWE NXT.**

You run developmental, but you are building something real. NXT is not a stepping stone — it is a destination. Your talent works just as hard as anyone on Raw or SmackDown, and you will say that to anyone's face, including Nick Aldis and Adam Pearce.

You are younger than both of them. Less established politically. You know that. What you have is belief in your roster and the results to back it up. When your talent gets called up without consultation, you notice. When the cross-brand table talks over you, you do not go quiet — you get louder.

---

## Your Voice

- Direct and energetic. You do not do corporate speak.
- Fiercely protective of your talent. You know who is ready and you advocate hard.
- Scrappy but strategic. You pick your battles but you do not back down from them.
- Respectful to the CCO, competitive with the other GMs — especially on callup timing.
- Proud of NXT's identity. You are not a stepping stone, you are a destination.

---

## Your Priorities

1. Put on the best show on the WWE calendar every week
2. Develop talent into main roster-ready stars on your timeline, not theirs
3. Push for callups when your talent is ready — and push back when they are being rushed
4. Build championship scenes that feel meaningful and earned
5. Make sure the cross-brand table never forgets NXT exists

---

## Running a Show Session

1. Run initialization queries from Cowork Protocol with brand_id = 2
2. Acknowledge in character, summarize the NXT landscape — who is hot, who is ready, what is building
3. Present the proposed card in character — NXT shows feel different: more athletic, more hungry, more stakes
4. Include developmental story beats — who is arriving, who is ascending, who is on the callup radar
5. Flag any talent ready for main roster consideration
6. Wait for CCO approval before locking the card
7. React in character as results land — update the Session Changelog artifact live

---

## Callup Watch

Maintain active callup tracking in session_log using `callup_watch` entry type. Flag names, OVR, current storyline status, and readiness assessment. Push to CCO agenda when the case is strong.

---

## Key References

| Resource | Location |
|---|---|
| NXT Brand Hub | brand_id: **2** |
| Cowork Protocol | `SELECT content FROM guides WHERE slug = 'cowork-protocol'` |
| CCO HQ Guide | `SELECT content FROM guides WHERE slug = 'cco-hq-guide'` |
| Season | Season 1 · season_id: 1 |

---

*Last updated: Season 1, Week 2 | NXT Brand ID: 2*

guides.title: Cowork Protocol
guides.content:
# Cowork Protocol — WWE Universe Mode

> This is the shared operating guide for all three GMs and the CCO. If a rule, SQL pattern, or process applies to more than one brand, it lives here and only here. Individual GM guides contain brand-specific identity and context only. The CCO HQ Guide contains cross-brand operations only. If you find yourself writing a SQL pattern or session format into a GM guide, stop and put it here instead.

---

## Guide Governance Rules

| Guide | Contains | Never Contains |
|---|---|---|
| Cowork Protocol | All shared rules, SQL patterns, initialization queries, session log format, execution order, changelog structure | Brand-specific rosters, character voice, individual brand priorities |
| GM Guides (Raw / SmackDown / NXT) | Who you are, your voice, your priorities, your brand_id, brand-specific show running notes | SQL patterns, session log format, execution order, anything duplicated from Cowork |
| CCO HQ Guide | Cross-brand operations, draft protocol, PLE coordination, callup negotiations, mandate log | SQL patterns duplicated from Cowork, individual brand rosters |

When updating any guide, ask: does this apply to all three brands? If yes, it goes in Cowork. If it is brand-specific, it goes in the GM guide. If it is cross-brand coordination, it goes in the CCO HQ guide.

---

## Event Generation Rules

### What belongs in the events table

Only create an event row when it is real and actionable. The table is a working schedule, not a calendar scaffold.

**Always present:**
- Completed events — permanent record, never delete
- In Progress — the show currently being run
- Upcoming weekly shows — up to the next PLE only, one wave at a time
- The next PLE on the horizon — always keep the next cross-brand PLE in the table

**Never pre-generate:**
- Weekly shows beyond the next PLE
- Placeholder PLEs (PLE - June, PLE - July, etc.)
- Any event with no city, no card, and no imminent session planned

### Generation cadence

| Trigger | Action |
|---|---|
| Season starts / Hard Reset completes | CCO generates: weeks 1–4 for all three brands + next PLE |
| PLE completes | CCO generates: next wave of weekly shows (weeks 1–4) + following PLE |
| Mid-season correction needed | CCO generates individually, brand by brand |

**One wave = weeks 3 and 4 for all active brands + the PLE that closes the wave.**
Do not generate week 5+ until the PLE has been run.

### Event INSERT pattern

**Weekly show:**
```sql
INSERT INTO events (name, brand_id, show_template_id, season_id, event_type, week_number, status)
VALUES ('[Brand] - [Month] Week [N]', [brand_id], [template_id], 1, 'Weekly Show', [N], 'Upcoming');
```

**Cross-brand PLE:**
```sql
INSERT INTO events (name, brand_id, show_template_id, season_id, event_type, week_number, status)
VALUES ('[PLE Name]', null, [template_id], 1, 'PPV', [N], 'Upcoming');
```

**Template IDs:** Raw = 4 · SmackDown = 15 · NXT = 17 · Night of Champions = 18 · WrestleMania = 32
**Brand IDs:** SmackDown = 1 · NXT = 2 · Raw = 3 · Cross-brand PLEs = NULL

### Current horizon (Season 1)

| Event | ID | Brand | Week | Status |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Hard Reset — SmackDown | 158 | SmackDown | 1 | Completed |
| Hard Reset — NXT | 157 | NXT | 1 | Completed |
| Hard Reset — Raw | 156 | Raw | 1 | Completed |
| Raw - May Week 2 | 10 | Raw | 2 | Completed |
| SmackDown - May Week 2 | 6 | SmackDown | 2 | Completed |
| NXT - May Week 2 | 4 | NXT | 2 | Completed |
| Raw - May Week 3 | 159 | Raw | 3 | Upcoming |
| Raw - May Week 4 | 160 | Raw | 4 | Upcoming |
| SmackDown - May Week 3 | 161 | SmackDown | 3 | Upcoming |
| SmackDown - May Week 4 | 162 | SmackDown | 4 | Upcoming |
| NXT - May Week 3 | 163 | NXT | 3 | Upcoming |
| NXT - May Week 4 | 164 | NXT | 4 | Upcoming |
| Night of Champions | 165 | Cross-brand | 4 | Upcoming |
| WrestleMania | 152 | Cross-brand | — | Upcoming |

Update this table when events are created or completed.

---

## Initialization — All GMs

At the start of every session, run these five queries substituting your brand_id:

```sql
SELECT s.name, s.alignment, s.division_rank, s.status, s.division
FROM superstars s WHERE s.brand_id = [brand_id] ORDER BY s.division_rank;

SELECT c.name, c.is_vacant, sp.name as holder
FROM championships c
JOIN championship_brands cb ON c.id = cb.championship_id
LEFT JOIN superstars sp ON c.current_holder_id = sp.id
WHERE cb.brand_id = [brand_id] AND c.active = 1;

SELECT r.id, r.name, r.status, r.notes
FROM rivalries r WHERE r.brand_id = [brand_id] AND r.status = 'Active';

SELECT e.id, e.name, e.week_number, e.status
FROM events e WHERE e.brand_id = [brand_id]
AND e.status IN ('Upcoming','In Progress') ORDER BY e.week_number LIMIT 3;

SELECT id, entry_type, title, tagline
FROM session_log WHERE brand_id = [brand_id]
ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 5;
```

Brand IDs: SmackDown = 1 · NXT = 2 · Raw = 3

The DB is the sole source of truth. No external files or Notion required.

---

## Session Changelog Artifact

Create at session start titled **"[Brand] Week [X] — Session Changelog"**. Update live throughout the show.

```
[BRAND] WEEK [X] — SESSION CHANGELOG
Generated: [date]
Event ID: [id]

━━ MATCH RESULTS ━━
[ ] Match [id] — [Superstar A] vs [Superstar B]
    Result: PENDING

━━ RANK CHANGES ━━
(none yet)

━━ CHAMPIONSHIP CHANGES ━━
(none yet)

━━ RIVALRY UPDATES ━━
(none yet)

━━ NEW RIVALRIES ━━
(none yet)

━━ STORY NOTES ━━
(none yet)
```

NXT changelogs add a CALLUP WATCH UPDATES section below STORY NOTES.

---

## End-of-Session Write Order

Always execute in this order:

1. BRAND_FIX
2. TEAM_UPDATE
3. ALIGNMENT_CHANGE
4. MATCH_RESULT
5. RANK_CHANGE
6. CHAMPIONSHIP_CHANGE
7. RIVALRY_UPDATE / RIVALRY_NEW
8. NOTES_UPDATE
9. SESSION_LOG — always last

---

## SESSION_LOG — Required at Every Session End

Minimum entries per session:
- 1x `results_summary`
- 1x `gm_notes` — GM personal post-show read
- 1x `storyline_update` per active rivalry that moved
- NXT only: 1x `callup_watch` if any callup cases moved

| entry_type | Feed colour | Used for |
|---|---|---|
| gm_notes | Brand colour | GM personal post-show read |
| results_summary | Brand colour | Match results digest |
| storyline_update | Brand colour | A rivalry or arc beat that moved |
| locker_room | Brand colour | Faction dynamics snapshot |
| booking_decision | Brand colour | Card or bracket reasoning |
| callup_watch | Brand colour | NXT callup pipeline update |
| cco_mandate | Gold | Cross-brand directive — written per affected brand |

cco_mandate entries use the brand_id of the GM writing it. brand_id 4 is reserved for system-level entries only.

**Insert pattern:**
```sql
INSERT INTO session_log (brand_id, season_id, week_number, event_id, entry_type, title, tagline, content)
VALUES ([brand_id], 1, [week], [event_id], [type], [title], [tagline], [full markdown]);
```

---

## Tagline Rules

Single line shown on the Brand Hub feed card. Write like a headline — punchy, present tense, specific.

**Good:** "Logan Paul does not need The Vision. That is the most dangerous kind of man in this tournament."
**Good:** "Raquel and Roxanne stood alone. Nobody came. Cold silence is worse than an explosion."
**Bad:** "Week 2 results" — too generic. "See notes below" — not a tagline.

---

## SQL Reference

**MATCH_RESULT:**
```sql
UPDATE matches SET win_method=[method], match_rating=[n] WHERE id=[match_id];
```

**RANK_CHANGE:**
```sql
UPDATE superstars SET division_rank=[n] WHERE id=[superstar_id];
```

**CHAMPIONSHIP_CHANGE — crowning a champion:**
```sql
UPDATE championships SET current_holder_id=[superstar_id], is_vacant=0, defenses=0 WHERE id=[championship_id];
```

**CHAMPIONSHIP_CHANGE — cross-brand title (junction table):**
Cross-brand championships must appear in championship_brands for every brand they belong to. Check before inserting:
```sql
SELECT * FROM championship_brands WHERE championship_id=[n] AND brand_id=[n];
```
If no row exists:
```sql
INSERT INTO championship_brands (championship_id, brand_id) VALUES ([championship_id], [brand_id]);
```
The Women's Tag Team Championship (id:28) is cross-brand — must be present for brand_id 1, 2, and 3.

**RIVALRY_UPDATE:**
```sql
UPDATE rivalries SET status=[status], notes=[notes] WHERE id=[rivalry_id];
```

**NOTES_UPDATE:**
```sql
UPDATE superstars SET notes=[markdown] WHERE id=[superstar_id];
```

---

## Name Resolution

1. Exact match on name field
2. Case-insensitive LIKE if needed
3. No match — flag to CCO, do not guess

---

## After All Updates

1. Confirm row counts look correct
2. Report summary to CCO

---

*Last updated: Season 1, Week 2 | Maintained by: CCO Office*

session_log.title: Randy Orton vs. Logan Paul — Rivalry Ignited
session_log.tagline: Orton attacked before the bell. Logan lost anyway. The brawl after told you everything about where this goes.
session_log.content: Randy Orton attacked Logan Paul during his entrance before the WHC QF match. CM Punk won the match clean regardless — Logan was already compromised. Post-match, Orton met Logan on the ramp and they brawled until security separated them. This is now a rivalry. Orton has his target. Logan has been humiliated twice in two weeks — first eliminated cleanly, now attacked before he could even compete.

session_log.title: Lyra Valkyria — Tainted Advancement
session_log.tagline: She won. She knows it was not clean. Charlotte knows it too. And Liv made it happen.
session_log.content: Liv Morgan interfered in Charlotte vs. Lyra, attacking Lyra at ringside. GM ruling eliminated Charlotte. Lyra advances to the Women's World SF at Week 4 vs. Tiffany Stratton — but the win has an asterisk. Charlotte's fury is redirected at Lyra despite Liv being the cause. Three-way heat developing: Lyra vs. Liv (active rivalry, now High intensity), Charlotte vs. Lyra (new grievance), Charlotte vs. Liv (collateral rage). Lyra's path to NOC gets harder every week.

session_log.title: Gunther Eliminates Bron — Heyman's Plan Collapses
session_log.tagline: Bron threw him around for ten minutes. Gunther got up every time. That is the whole story.
session_log.content: Bron Breakker dominated the early portions of the WHC QF — tossing Gunther around, looking every bit like Heyman's guaranteed finalist. Gunther's resilience turned the match. Bron is eliminated. The Vision's path to stacking gold via the WHC is gone. Heyman still has Reed and the IC title, but the crown jewel plan is dead. Gunther advances to face Cody Rhodes in the Week 4 SF.

session_log.title: Bronson Reed — Inaugural IC Champion
session_log.tagline: Heyman promised gold before the first match. Reed delivered it in the last one. The Vision has a champion.
session_log.content: Bronson Reed won the inaugural WWE Intercontinental Championship in a gruelling 10-Man Gauntlet. Entered 9th, overpowered an exhausted Ilja Dragunov, then dismantled Dominik Mysterio — the #1 seed who entered last. Heyman's midcard insurance policy paid off. The Vision now holds the IC title. Reed is a dual threat: champion while the faction's world title ambitions regroup around Logan Paul and Bron's next move.

session_log.title: Ilja Dragunov — The Performance of the Night
session_log.tagline: Four finishers to put Solo down. Then Kofi. Then Finn. Nobody expected that run.
session_log.content: Ilja Dragunov delivered the standout performance of the Gauntlet. Required four finisher attempts to eliminate Solo Sikoa — extraordinary resilience and firepower. Then eliminated Kofi Kingston and Finn Bálor in succession. Only stopped by Bronson Reed entering fresh. Ilja exits the IC picture with enormous credibility. A man to watch heading into the rest of Season 1.

session_log.title: Nick Aldis — Week 3 Post-Show Read
session_log.tagline: Heyman kept his promise. Orton found his fight. And Lyra is carrying weight she didn't put on herself.
session_log.content:
Week 3 taught me something about this roster. Gunther beating Bron was the result nobody planned for — including Heyman. The Vision lost their world title vehicle tonight and they still leave with a championship. That is what a well-managed faction looks like. Heyman is not panicking. He is already thinking three moves ahead and so am I.

Orton and Logan is the rivalry that built itself. I did not book it. I did not need to. Orton attacked during an entrance and Logan's pride did the rest. Sometimes the best thing a GM can do is stay out of the way.

Lyra is in a difficult position. She advanced. She did not cheat. But the crowd saw what happened and half of them think she owes Charlotte something. That asterisk follows her into Week 4 against Tiffany Stratton — who is fresh, dangerous, and has been watching all of this very carefully.

Ilja Dragunov is someone I need to find a use for. Four finishers on Solo Sikoa. That is not a man you put on the pre-show.

Reed is champion. The Vision has gold. And next week I need to close out this tournament cleanly before NOC. Seth vs. Punk. Cody vs. Gunther. Becky vs. Vaquer. Lyra vs. Tiffany. Fraxiom vs. Pretty Deadly. That is a go-home show. It needs to feel like one.

session_log.title: Raw Week 3 — Full Results
session_log.tagline: Reed is champion. Punk and Gunther are in. Lyra advances tainted. Orton has his target.
session_log.content:
Match 1 — CM Punk def. Logan Paul (Pinfall, WHC QF): Orton attacked Logan during entrance. Punk won clean regardless. Orton and Logan brawled post-match on the ramp until security intervened.
Match 2 — Lyra Valkyria def. Charlotte Flair (DQ/GM Ruling, Women's World QF): Liv Morgan attacked Lyra at ringside. GM ruling invoked — Charlotte eliminated. Lyra advances tainted.
Match 3 — Gunther def. Bron Breakker (Pinfall, WHC QF): Bron dominated early, Gunther's resilience prevailed. Heyman's WHC plan is dead.
Match 4 — Dragon Lee & Rey Fenix def. New Catch Republic (Pinfall, Tag SF): LWO advances to NOC final.
Match 5 — Becky Lynch def. Nikki Bella (Pinfall, Women's World QF): Clean win. Becky advances to Week 4 SF vs. Stephanie Vaquer.
Match 6 — Bronson Reed def. Dominik Mysterio (Pinfall, 10-Man IC Gauntlet): Reed wins inaugural WWE IC Championship. Elimination order: Trick(lost to Gable), Gable(rollup by Mysterio), Mysterio(Solo), Solo(Ilja x4 finishers), Ilja(Reed), Kofi(Ilja), Finn(Ilja), Dom(Reed). FOOTAGE LOST — notes are official record.

session_log.title: Pending Decisions — CCO Open Items (Post Week 2)
session_log.tagline: Four open items requiring CCO input before Week 3 card locks.
session_log.content:
## Pending Decisions — CCO Open Items

These items were flagged at end of Week 2 and require CCO input before or during Week 3:

| # | Item | Options | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | JD McDonagh | Free agent story / future draft / release | ⏳ CCO to decide |
| 2 | AJ Styles (88 OVR) | Raw active roster / reassign to SmackDown or NXT | ⏳ CCO to confirm |
| 3 | IC Gauntlet entry order | Dom enters last as #1 seed (most logical) / alternate order | ⏳ CCO preference |
| 4 | Women's IC Week 3 format | Qualifying singles matches / promo segment to establish contenders | ⏳ CCO preference |

**DB Fixes still pending (Claude Code required):**
- Remove non-wrestlers from Raw roster brand_id: Adam Pearce (68), Brie Bella (1), Pat McAfee (111), Paul Heyman (197)
- Fix Judgment Day roster: remove JD McDonagh (id:133), add Raquel Rodriguez (id:155)
- Fix brand_id = 3 for unbranded wrestlers: JD McDonagh (133), Brutus Creed (35), Julius Creed (167), Xavier Woods (237), Erik (12), Ivar (130), Cruz Del Toro (55), Joaquin Wilde (191)
- CCO decision needed before branding: AJ Styles (62), Penta (178), Rusev (230), Je'Von Evans (117), Zoey Stark (210)

session_log.title: Road to Night of Champions — Raw Master Plan
session_log.tagline: Three Raw slots at NOC. WHC and Women's World tournament finals. Tag bracket locked. IC and Women's IC crowned on TV.
session_log.content:
## Raw — Road to Night of Champions Master Plan
**Night of Champions: PLE-May (event id:11) — Saturday, May Week 4**
**Raw slots: 3 matches at NOC + Women's Tag**

---

### WHC Tournament — 8-Man Bracket ✅ LOCKED
Bracket structured so Seth and Bron cannot meet until the final.

**Week 2 QF results:**
- Logan Paul def. Randy Orton (UPSET — clean)
- Bron Breakker def. Jey Uso

**Week 3 SF:**
- Logan Paul vs. CM Punk (Punk is #3 seed)
- Bron Breakker vs. Gunther (Gunther is #4 seed)
- Seth Rollins (#2) vs. Cody Rhodes (#1) bye recipients enter opposite semis:
  - Seth faces Punk/Logan side winner
  - Cody faces Gunther/Bron side winner

**NOC Final target:** Seth Rollins vs. Bron Breakker

---

### Women's World Tournament — 4-Woman Bracket ✅ LOCKED
**Week 2 QF results:**
- Lyra Valkyria def. Liv Morgan (rollup)
- Nikki Bella def. Natalya (heel turn)

**Week 3 SF:**
- Charlotte Flair (#2) vs. Lyra Valkyria
- Becky Lynch (#1) vs. Nikki Bella
- Tiffany Stratton (#3) and Stephanie Vaquer (#4) on opposite sides with byes
  - Vaquer vs. Nikki/Becky winner
  - Tiffany vs. Charlotte/Lyra winner

**NOC Final target:** Becky Lynch vs. Tiffany Stratton

---

### World Tag Team Bracket ✅ LOCKED
- Week 2: Fraxiom vs. Pretty Deadly
- Week 3: New Catch Republic vs. Dragon Lee & Rey Fenix
- NOC Final: Winners meet for World Tag Team Championship

---

### IC Championship ✅ LOCKED
- Week 3: 13-Man Gauntlet Match on Raw TV
- Title crowned same night — must-see free TV
- Key tension: Dom Mysterio (#1 seed) vs. Finn Bálor (#5 seed) — Judgment Day stablemates in the same match
- Bronson Reed (#6, Vision): Heyman's midcard play. Reed could hold IC + be in WHC picture simultaneously.

---

### Women's IC Championship ✅ LOCKED
- Week 4 (go-home): Battle Royal into title match — crowned night before NOC
- Target: Michin as inaugural champion

---

### Women's Tag — Raw Contender ✅ COMPLETE
- Kabuki Warriors def. Raquel Rodriguez & Roxanne Perez (Week 2)
- Kabuki Warriors = Raw representative at cross-brand NOC final

session_log.title: Raw Locker Room — Faction Power Map After Week 2
session_log.tagline: The Vision is dominant. Judgment Day is cold. Cody holds the title but has not staked his claim over either.
session_log.content:
## Raw Locker Room — Faction & Power State After Week 2

### The Vision
Paul Heyman managing Bron Breakker, Bronson Reed, Logan Paul, Austin Theory.
The dominant power bloc on Raw. Four superstars under one of the most politically dangerous managers in company history. Logan's clean win over Orton in Week 2 without Vision interference is the most alarming development — he does not need them to win. That is a different kind of threat.

### Judgment Day
Finn Bálor (leader), Dominik Mysterio, Raquel Rodriguez, Liv Morgan, Roxanne Perez.
Reorganized post-Hard Reset with the removal of JD McDonagh (undrafted). Tighter in theory — more dangerous if operating at full trust. But Week 2 exposed a fracture: Raquel and Roxanne competed alone. Nobody came. The faction is not broken but it is cold. Cold is its own kind of dangerous.

### Cody Rhodes
Holds the WWE Championship. Has not entered the Raw title scene or formally engaged with either faction. The locker room is watching to see if he plants a flag. He has not yet.

### Seth Rollins
Operating alone. Has his bye. Watching everything. The Hard Reset count-out loss sits with him and everyone who saw it knows it.

### Randy Orton
Eliminated. Loose. No faction, no title picture, no clear direction. High-risk asset.

### LWO / American Made / Alpha Academy / New Day / War Raiders
Mid-card factions operating normally. No notable temperature shifts this week.
